flashcards.definition: The process of mountain building, typically by tectonic forces (folding, faulting, and uplift).
Mountains formed by compressional forces folding rock layers upward (e.g., Himalayas, Alps).
Mountains formed when tensional forces cause blocks of rock to be uplifted along normal faults.
An upward (arch-shaped) fold in rock layers; oldest rocks at the center.
A downward (trough-shaped) fold in rock layers; youngest rocks at the center.
